revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Izawa-jinja]][[ja:伊射波神社]][[zh:伊射波神社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Toba, Mie, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izawa Jinja
伊射波神社 | image = Izawa jinja Haiden.JPG | caption = Izawa Jinja Haiden {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Mie Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|28|22.44|N|136|52|27.66|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline, title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Wakahiru-me]] | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location = 1210 Arashimacho, Toba-shi, Mie-ken | website = | architecture_style = ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Izawa Jinja'''|伊射波神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the Arashima neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Toba, Mie|en|Toba, Mie|qid=Q819629|lt=Toba|1=Toba, Mie}} in {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is one of the two shrines claiming the title of ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former {{ill|Shima Province|en|Shima Province|qid=Q1053722|lt=Shima Province|1=Shima Province}}. The main festivals of the shrine are held annually on January 9, June 7 and November 23. It is also referred to as the {{nihongo|'''Shima Daimyōjin'''|志摩大明神|}}.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Izawa Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Wakahiru-me]]-no-mikoto'''| 稚日女尊 |}}, daughter or younger sister of [[Amaterasu]] * {{nihongo|'''Izawatomi-no-mikoto'''| 伊佐波登美命 |}}, local ''kami'' who greeted {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=PrincessYamato|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} when she was looking for a site for this shrine * {{nihongo|'''Tamahashiraya-hime-no-nikoto'''| 玉柱屋姫命 |}}, ancestor of the Ise ''[[kuni no miyatsuko]]'' * {{nihongo|'''Sayori-hime-no-mikoto'''| 狭依姫命 |}}, territorial goddess of an island offshore Shima, now submerged. ==History== The origins of the Izawa Jinja are unknown. The shrine claims to have a history extending over 1500 years, and was originally the maritime guardian deity of Shima Province centered on the worship of Wakahiru-me for protection of travelers on the sea route across {{ill|Ise Bay|en|Ise Bay|qid=Q1368308|lt=Ise Bay|1=Ise Bay}} which was part of the ancient route of the {{ill|Tōkaidō (road)|en|Tōkaidō (road)|qid=Q963514|lt=Tōkaidō|1=Tōkaidō (road)}} highway to eastern Japan. The shrine appears in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' in 927 AD; however, there is controversy as to whether the shrine listed in the ''Engishiki'' (and in other ancient records), is this shrine, or if it is the rival [[Izawa-no-miya]]. The Izawa Jinja has claimed to be the ''ichinomiya'' of Shima Province from the early {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}. One justification is that the shrine listed in the ''[[Wamyō Ruijushō]]'' dictionary of 938 is clearly this shrine and not the Izawa-no-miya based on the ''{{ill|Kanji|en|Kanji|qid=Q82772|lt=Kanji|1=Kanji}}'' used in its name and the location listed. Another is that Izawa-no-miya is not an independent shrine, but is a branch of the [[Ise Grand Shrine]] and {{ill|Toba Domain|en|Toba Domain|qid=Q1396816|lt=Toba Domain|1=Toba Domain}} in the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} sponsored this shrine as the ''ichinomiya'' as a rival to the Izawa-no-miya as Ise Grand Shrine was not in their territory. A counter argument is that by definition, the ''ichinomiya'' of a province much be located in close proximity to its [[kokufu|provincial capital]], and Izawa-no-miya fits this description better than Izawa Jinja. In any event, the shrine was destroyed and rebuilt several times in its history, especially during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} and it was also once destroyed by a {{ill|Tsunami|en|Tsunami|qid=Q8070|lt=Tsunami|1=Tsunami}} in the {{ill|1854 Tōkai earthquake|en|1854 Tōkai earthquake|qid=Q3742363|lt=1854 Tōkai earthquake|1=1854 Tōkai earthquake}}, and all of its records were lost. During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} era of {{ill|State Shinto|en|State Shinto|qid=Q1197248|lt=State Shinto|1=State Shinto}}, the shrine was not given a Rank under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]].{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} The shrine is located a five-minute walk from {{ill|Kaminogō Station|en|Kaminogō Station|qid=Q1447297|lt=Kaminogō Station|1=Kaminogō Station}} on the [[:en:Kintetsu Shima Line|Kintetsu Shima Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== Kaburako Promontory.JPG|Kaburako Promontory Izawa jinja Gate.JPG|Ichi-no-Torii Izawa jinja Honden.JPG|Honden ==See also== *{{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|qid=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}, Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commonscat| Izawa-jinja}} *[https://www.iseshima-kanko.jp/spot/1560 Ise Shime Convention & Tourism official site] *[https://www.isejingu.or.jp/about/naiku/izawa.html Ise Jingu Official site]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_22168.html Mie Prefecture Official tourist information site] {{in lang|ja}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Ichinomiya}} [[en:Izawa-jinja]] {{Commons category|Izawa-jinja}} [[ja:伊射波神社]] [[zh:伊射波神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11379325}} [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shima Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Toba, Mie]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
